CHAPTER 1. PREPARING TO INSTALL WITH THE AGENT-BASED INSTALLER
 1.2.1. Agent-based Installer workflow
 One of the control plane hosts runs the Assisted Service at the start of the boot process and eventually
becomes the bootstrap host. This node is called the rendezvous host (node 0). The Assisted Service
ensures that all the hosts meet the requirements and triggers an OpenShift Container Platform cluster
deployment. All the nodes have the Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS (RHCOS) image written to the
disk. The non-bootstrap nodes reboot and initiate a cluster deployment. Once the nodes are rebooted,
the rendezvous host reboots and joins the cluster. The bootstrapping is complete and the cluster is
deployed.
 Figure 1.1. Node installation workflow
 You can install a disconnected OpenShift Container Platform cluster through the openshift-install 
agent create image subcommand for the following topologies:
 A single-node OpenShift Container Platform cluster (SNO): A node that is both a master and
worker.
 A three-node OpenShift Container Platform cluster : A compact cluster that has three
